question_text: Two springs, A and B, are hanging vertically with identical masses attached. Spring A has a spring constant of 200 N/m and spring B has a spring constant of 100 N/m. Compare the extensions of the two springs.
correct_answer: Spring B extends twice as much as Spring A. Since the force is the same (both support equal masses), and F = kx, a smaller spring constant means a larger extension. Spring B's constant is half that of Spring A, so its extension is doubled.
explanation: Using F = kx with the same force on both springs, a smaller spring constant produces a larger extension. Spring B (k = 100 N/m) will stretch twice as much as Spring A (k = 200 N/m) because they experience the same weight force.
